channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
item.description: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.
item.content:encoded:
In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: No Deal: U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapse
item.description: The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement after a day of highly anticipated face-to-face peace talks, Washington's lead negotiator Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement after a day of highly anticipated face-to-face peace talks, Washington's lead negotiator Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Sunday.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: The real space science behind 'Project Hail Mary'
item.description: The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.
item.content:encoded:
The science fiction blockbuster wowed audiences with its depiction of space travel and more. Here's what NASA staff and other scientists say about the basis for the amazing events of the film.

(Image credit: Jonathan Olley)
item.dc:creator: Tara Haelle

item.title: White House ballroom construction can continue for now, appeals court says
item.description: The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.
item.content:encoded:
The order comes as the Trump administration challenges a lower court ruling that the estimated $300-million project requires congressional approval.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Trump touts newly released plans for D.C. triumphal arch
item.description: The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.
item.content:encoded:
The proposed 250-feet-tall, white-and-gilded monument would stand on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., by the Potomac River.

(Image credit: Jon Elswick)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: At a concert in Budapest, anti-Orbán sentiments take center stage ahead of election
item.description: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.
item.content:encoded: At a concert in Budapest, musicians and concertgoers express criticism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's leadership.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: How Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's hometown became a symbol of excesses
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.
item.content:encoded:
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has long been accused of corruption. Sightseers now flock to his hometown as groups aim to raise awareness of what they say are the leader's excesses.

(Image credit: Rob Schmitz)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: In Hungary, upcoming elections could bring an end to Orban's 16-year rule
item.description: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.
item.content:encoded: In Hungary, voters head to the polls Sunday. At stake: the future for populist Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, Europe's longest-serving leader - and an ally of Presidents Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran
item.description: In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his criticism starting on Palm Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his criticism starting on Palm Sunday.

(Image credit: Gregorio Borgia)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Artemis II splashdown captures nationwide attention
item.description: Fans across the country tuned in to see the Artemis II crew make their splashy return to Earth.
item.content:encoded:
Fans across the country tuned in to see the Artemis II crew make their splashy return to Earth.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
